reasoning: The FRED tool was unable to retrieve data, thus I must rely on macroeconomic context and historical behaviors. Commercial bank MBS holdings have broadly trended downwards since the 2022 rate hikes and subsequent balance sheet realignments. With the Fed cutting rates in late 2024 through 2025, prepayment rates on MBS accelerate, causing the MBS portfolio on bank balance sheets to naturally run off faster. Without aggressive reinvestment—which banks are likely avoiding given enhanced regulatory focus on interest rate risk (post-SVB)—the balance tends to drift lower week-over-week. Given the downward drift, the probability of seeing an outstanding balance higher than the 2025-10-26 baseline is less than 50%. As explicitly instructed, I must make uncertainty increase over longer forecasting horizons, meaning my probabilities must move closer to 0.5 for the more distant dates, starting with a lower base certainty for the nearest resolution date.
